title: ophioglossum pycnostichum
intro: Ophioglossum pycnostichum, also known as Adder's Tongue or Adder's Tongue Fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the Ophioglossaceae family. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ophioglossum pycnostichum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and soothe digestive problems. Ophioglossum pycnostichum is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and is sometimes dried and used in herbal supplements, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
